coach_note: Welcome to Block 2 — a 10-week Norwegian Singles base build (26 May → 2 Aug) laying the foundation for Berlin Marathon. Block 1 was a triumph: CTL up from 19 to 36, VO2max nudged 49→51, and the 5×3km benchmark plus 24km long run showed the system is responding. Now we step up. Tuesdays alternate: odd weeks 3×10-12min @ 30K pace (4:34-4:42/km), even weeks 5-6×6min @ HM pace (4:28-4:36/km). Thursdays are 30/30s @ 15K pace (4:22-4:30/km), growing from 2×10 to 18+18 reps across the block — anything above 20 splits into two sets with 2min rest. Long run climbs +1km/wk from 22 to 30 (capped) — this is where Berlin gets won. Sub-T quality capped at 36min per session to keep the build sustainable. The non-negotiable add-on: two strength sessions per week. Lorcan has invested in equipment for a reason — the last 10km of Berlin is where unsupported runners fade. Bulletproofing the body now means he holds form when others crumble. S&C is not optional supplementary work; it's load-bearing infrastructure. Easy days must be genuinely easy (<148 bpm) — this was a flag in Block 1 and the bigger volumes this block make it more important, not less. Trust the pace targets, trust the easy days, trust the strength work.

predictions.rationale: Block 1 base building delivered ahead of schedule — CTL nearly doubled (19→36), VO2max trending up, sub-threshold sessions executing well at target HR. Block 2 builds the marathon-specific endurance with long runs to 30km and continues sub-T development. Adding 2x/week strength work to bulletproof against late-race fade, which is the primary risk factor for Berlin given Lorcan's previous Valencia DNF. With 18 weeks until race day after this block, there's room for a dedicated 6-8 week marathon-specific phase post-base. On track for sub-3:20.
